reasoning_model_1: Model 1 strictly follows the content requirements, accurately synthesizing the text '这么早就要起床，真是太难了。'. Crucially, it effectively incorporates the required paralinguistic feature (non-verbal vocalization). The audio begins at 00:00 with a distinct, natural-sounding yawn (audible inhalation and breathy exhalation) that lasts until 00:01. Following this, the speech from 00:01 to 00:05 is delivered with a lethargic, tired prosody, maintaining the 'sleepy' persona consistent with having just yawned. The breathiness and slower pace perfectly match the command.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 fails on multiple fronts. Firstly, regarding Content Accuracy, it hallucinates and alters the text significantly. Instead of the requested sentence, it says '哈，这么早就要起床，真的好难啊，眼皮都快粘一起了。' (Ha, getting up so early is really hard, my eyelids are sticking together). Secondly, regarding the paralinguistic feature, it does not produce a non-verbal yawn. It simply reads the character 'Ha' (哈) at 00:00 in a bright, energetic, and 'cute' tone. The overall prosody is upbeat and awake, which is the direct opposite of a 'yawning manner' or sleepy state. There is no non-verbal yawning sound effect.
model_comparison: Model 1 is the clear winner. It accurately reproduces the requested text and successfully generates the specific non-verbal vocalization (a realistic yawn) at the beginning, integrating it naturally with a sleepy speaking style. Model 2 fails to follow the text constraint (adding a whole new clause) and completely fails to generate the requested paralinguistic feature. Instead of a yawn, Model 2 provides an energetic spoken syllable and an upbeat tone that contradicts the context of the prompt.
